text: getting a moody, potbellied pig out of his stall so you can muck it out... It’s not easy.” Luke laughs. “We got it, Tris.” A smile lights up inside me at the use of Luke’s nickname for me. I’m Mama Bear to Calder and Tris to Luke. Nicknames are such an odd, comforting thing. Like I’m finally one of the family. “Thank you both, again.” I inhale sharply as a shooting pain courses through my belly. “You okay?” Luke stares down at me with worry. I wave him off. “Yes, I’m fine. It’s just the damn Braxton-Hicks. They’re worse than ever today.” They both continue to gape at me, but I ignore them and get back to the task at hand. “And I know you guys are coming to Max’s tomorrow for Christmas.” A harsh breath escapes my lips as pain shoots through my groin. “But remind me again that you can get back up the mountain if the snow gest really bad.” My bajingo radiates a deep ache that causes me to hunch over and grip the edge of Reggie’s pen. “Seriously, Tris, this isn’t normal.” Luke rushes over to me and places a hand on my back. I slap the wood of Reggie’s gate. “Just tell me you can get up this mountain in a storm!” “Yes, the snowmobiles are stored in the shed at the bottom of the lane,” Calder says on a rush, gripping his neck and eyeing me cautiously from a safe distance. “We know how to get up and down if needed. Now you need to get to that truck before Wyatt—” “Oh my God,” I gasp when I feel an intense wave of pressure hit me out of nowhere that’s immediately followed by a popping sensation. “I just peed my pants!” “You what?” Calder’s face is the picture of disturbed. “Oh my God, I peed my pants,” I exclaim with irritation. “I did not read that in the baby books! Ow!” Another sharp sensation roils through me, and I hunch over, tensing through something fierce. “Tris, I don’t think you peed your pants,” Luke says, looking down at my soaked jeans. “I think your water broke.” “Oh shit!” I shout as realization dawns on me. “Wyatt is going to be so mad at youuuu,” Calder sings, a cocky glint in his eye as he looks at me all hunched over and wet. “Shut up, Calder!” Luke and I both erupt in unison. “I’m just saying...he wanted you off this mountain for this exact reason, and now we’re literally about ready to have a baby Jesus in the barn on Christmas Eve.” “We are not!” I begin to make my way out of the barn, and Luke and Calder instantly rush to my sides, grabbing both arms like I’m incapable of walking. “Stop touching me. I can walk just fine on my own!” “What happened?” Wyatt roars as he sees me walking out of the barn like a total spectacle. “Mama Bear is about to have your cub,” Calder replies with a laugh. “What?” Wyatt roars with wide, terrified eyes on me. “Relax, Wyatt...it was just my water breaking.” “Holy shit!” he exclaims, and my body is jostled when he swoops me up into his arms. “I don’t need to be carried!” I argue as he jogs...literally jogs...toward the truck. He opens the back door for me while barking commands at his brothers. “Luke, you drive. Calder...you stay with the animals.” “No way am I missing this!” Calder argues as he jumps in the passenger side. “What about the animals?” I look at Calder with pleading eyes. “We just fed them. They’ll be fine until morning.” “And you guys swear you can get back up the mountain?” “Yes!” All three brothers bark at me in unison. “Then, Luke...please drive like hell because it feels like I’m about to give birth in this truck!” Wyatt begins muttering obscenities under his breath, and I know it has everything to do with the fact that he wanted to be in Boulder when this happened, not up on Mount Millie in the middle of a snowstorm. But where would the fun be in that? “Oh God,” I cry out when a wave of contractions hits me. “This hurts so bad!” Wyatt moves sideways on the seat, pulling me in between his legs and holding me close. “Lean on me and squeeze my hand, baby.” I do so, but it does nothing to stop the intense feeling pressing down on my asshole. “It feels like I need to poop.” Calder’s head snaps around to look back at me nervously. “The books said that’s how it feels right before you need to start pushing,” Wyatt says, his voice fraught with worry. “No fucking pushing!” Calder cries, turning forward and running his hand through his hair. “Luke, switch places with me. I want to drive. You can deliver the baby.” “No one is delivering the baby in this truck!” I shriek, gripping Wyatt’s leg for dear life. “Ugh, I should have listened to you, Wyatt.” “I know.” “I am so stubborn.” “I know.” “I always have to push the limits.” “I know.” “You could disagree with me on one of these, you know!” “I... Okay...you aren’t stubborn.” “You’re such a bad liar,” I whine, and a weird humming noise erupts from my chest that I’ve never heard before. “You need to drive faster, Luke,” Wyatt begs. “Do you want to get there safely or not?” Luke snarls and it’s the most heated I’ve ever seen him. His voice is low when I hear him murmur, “Why am I always the one driving to the hospitals?” I close my eyes and try my best to stay calm as we make our way down the winding roads of rural Colorado to the Boulder Medical Center. The doctor wanted to induce me a week ago, but I said no. I don’t want drugs for the delivery, and induction requires drugs. Wyatt’s right, I am
